staff.name: Carl Vitense
staff.bio:
Carl is a Licensed Psychotherapist with over 20 years experience doing transformational work with people of all ages and backgrounds.  He has worked throughout the mental health field, serving in residential treatment, juvenile corrections diversion, in-home family preservation, CCS (Comprehensive Community Services), private outpatient practice, and as a university social work professor/field liaison.  

Having fostered 18 boys over 8 years as a treatment foster care parent, Carl has signature experience in issues surrounding out of home care and adoption as well as developmental disorders.  Carl provides assessment and treatment for PTSD, depression, anxiety, ADHD, and schizophrenia/psychosis.  He also has particular interest in parent coaching/family therapy, men’s issues, character education, leadership/personal performance enhancement, and creativity.  

Carl works from a strengths based and humanistic core philosophy that is primarily informed by Jungian analytical psychology (complexes, dreams, the unconscious), trauma therapy/EMDR, spiritual, and existential approaches.  His work is also influenced by positive psychology/resilience, narrative work, ecopsychology, and experiential models (art, sand tray, and outdoor challenge).

In addition to individual therapy, Carl is available to other professionals and agencies for clinical supervision, case consultation, training, and program development.  

Carl has traveled to over 40 countries with his most inspiring journey having made the Camino Santiago pilgrimage in 2019.  He enjoys hiking, mountains, nature/animals, history, myth and fairy tales, writing, photography and all things nerdy (anime, sci-fi, fantasy, board games, and tabletop RPGs like D&D).

As the Founder and Clinical Director of Inner Journey Consulting, Carl is dedicated to assisting individuals on their inner journey towards deeper wholeness and integration while being of service to the Milwaukee and greater SE Wiscsonsin community. 

Education/Key Training:

Analyst Training Program (ATP) Candidate
C.G. Jung Institute of Chicago, 2023 - In Process

Jungian Psychotherapy Program (JPP) Certificate
CG Jung Institute of Chicago, 2018

Masters of Social Work (MSW): Double Methods and
Certificate in Nonprofit Management
University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee, 2008

Bachelor's Degree in History and Theology, Minor in Psychology
Wisconsin Lutheran College, 2002

Trauma Focused: 

Eye Movement Desensitization Reprocessing (EMDR) Training Certificate

Neuro-Sequential Model of Therapeutics (NMT) trained

Trauma Focused-Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (TF-CBT) Certificate

staff.name: Sophia Klingbiel
staff.bio:
Sophia Klingbiel is a LCSW and has been providing therapy and psychoeducation since 2012. Sophia is a graduate of George Williams College where she earned her Masters in Social Work. Her prior education earned her a Bachelor's degree in Social Work from Mount Mary College. Sophia's main focus is in trauma, depression amd anxiety. She also works with ADHD, adoption, life skills, and other mental health needs. 

Sophia is fully trained in EMDR, has begun training in Brainspotting and uses CBT, Mindfulness, Nature and art within her practice. Sophia believes in helping her clients express their needs in therapy as she believes that the client knows what they need and she can provide the support and guidance. 

In her spare time Sophia enjoys spending time with her family out in nature exploring and creating. Sophia enjoys travel, meeting new people and having new experiences. She enjoys education and believes that every day holds a new opportunity to learn something new.

staff.name: Andrew Arrowood
staff.bio:
Working on yourself is a lifelong process.

Let’s take it a day at a time, a goal at a time, and together we can work towards becoming your best self.

Life is a marathon, not a race.

staff.name: Grant Clark
staff.bio:
Hi, I’m Grant Clark, a psychotherapist with a background working with teens, adults, and elderly clients, with an emphasis on men and adolescents, primarily navigating identity-related concerns, depression, ADHD, and anxiety.

I take a neurodivergent-affirming and trauma-focused approach working with my clients, primarily using Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) and Existential Therapy. In sessions, this means helping clients develop more flexible ways of responding to difficult thoughts, emotions, and life challenges by identifying personal values and meaning so that they can take steps to live a more fulfilling life. I also incorporate other modalities like Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT) and somatic-based approaches to provide skills when appropriate.

Outside of the office, I spend most of my time doing various arts and crafts, playing games, working out, or being out in nature.

staff.name: Thoy Bouakongxaya
staff.bio:
Coming from a refugee family, my family needed a lot of help and guidance upon arriving in the US. Little did I know that would instill a deep desire to help others as others have helped me along my journey. Anytime you want to do better, be better, make changes for yourself, it will require hard work but it is not impossible to reach your goals you have for yourself. I believe that everyday you wake up, you have a chance to be a better you!

I have experience working with children, adolescents and families in a therapeutic setting. I have worked with individuals experiencing anxiety, depression, suicidal ideation, relationship challenges, communication struggles among other well being concerns. 

I am familiar with different therapy models and understand that one model does not fit all. I am here to work with you to figure out a person centered plan.

staff.name: Michelle Townsend de López
staff.bio: A highly skilled bilingual professional, adept at navigating diverse cultural and faith-based environments and communicating fluidly in customs and culture. A strong educational background and extensive experience in both domestic and international settings, I demonstrate exceptional adaptability and a keen understanding of navigating the realities in urban, suburban and rural contexts. A strong commitment to empower individuals to deconstruct the biases and negative narratives and gain tools to reconstruct your lived reality with grace and strength. Our lives are about learning to flourish in difficult circumstances and still show up and appreciate the becoming of your best self.

staff.name: Lily Guth
staff.bio: Hi, I’m Lily (she/her), a BIPOC adoptee and certified social worker with experience ranging from child protection services to supporting adults with disabilities and children with special needs. I’m a big foodie, animal lover, and outdoors enthusiast. I’m married with a blended family, including two wonderful children with special needs, and I’ll be starting my Master of Social Work at the University of Denver in 2026. I’m truly looking forward to connecting and working with you!

staff.name: Tim Nett
staff.bio:
Tim Nett earned a Bachelor of Science in Psychology from St. Norbert College and a Master of Science in Family Relations & Child Development, Marriage & Family Therapy from Oklahoma State University. In addition to his clinical training, Tim brings more than 20 years of experience in Human Resources, giving him a deep understanding of how individuals, families, workplaces, and systems shape our lives and relationships.

As the youngest of ten children and a father himself, Tim understands the complexity of family dynamics, relationships, resilience, and personal growth. Raised in a family business focused on residential construction, he developed a lifelong appreciation for creating, building, and problem-solving—whether through remodeling, landscaping, or helping people rebuild and strengthen their lives.

In 2018, Tim was diagnosed with Parkinson’s disease, a journey that has deepened his empathy, resilience, and perspective. While Parkinson’s is part of his story, it does not define it. Tim is an active advocate, volunteer, and ambassador, helping raise awareness, support, education, and research funding for individuals and families impacted by Parkinson’s disease.

Tim’s creativity extends into music, where he performs classic and popular songs as a vocalist, saxophonist, and harmonica player. His love for music reflects the same energy, authenticity, and human connection he values in his work with clients.

Known for his compassionate, grounded, and often playful approach, Tim believes meaningful change begins when people feel genuinely seen, heard, and understood. He works collaboratively with clients, drawing from their strengths, experiences, and goals to support healing, growth, and lasting change.

Tim believes that growth happens in relationships, healing begins with connection, and no one should have to navigate life’s challenges alone.

staff.name: Matt Becker
staff.bio:
Matt has a BA in fine arts and worked as a graphic designer for over 20 years. He decided to switch to helping children and families, and became certified to do so. He has volunteered with various organizations, including Artists Working in Education, which went to different Milwaukee County parks and did art projects with kids.

Matt is dedicated to helping families and individuals develop skills in problem solving, self-awareness, communication and self-regulation. He is also dedicated to helping individuals with real life skills and independent living skills. He is interested in using art to help foster skills and communicate emotions and feelings, as well as a creative outlet.

In his free time Matt enjoys creating art, particularly drawing; listening to music and going to concerts; spending time with family and friends; working on cars; reading; learning about history; and watching and playing basketball.

staff.name: Ari Dragovich
staff.bio:
As a professional deeply committed to helping others, I believe in meeting young people where they are and walking with them as they navigate life’s challenges and discover their own paths forward. I’m passionate about fostering spaces where youth feel safe to be curious, vulnerable, and fully themselves—spaces rooted in respect, connection, and genuine care. I draw on principles of trauma-informed care, solution-focused thinking, and honest self-examination to support emotional regulation, build trust, and encourage meaningful growth.

My approach is deeply informed by a wide range of disciplines—psychology, sociology, religion, mythology, history, and current culture—all of which help me better understand the forces that shape our lives and identities. At the heart of my work is a commitment to truth: helping others explore, name, and live into their own truths so they can lead lives that feel meaningful, empowered, and free.

Outside of work, I enjoy reading, writing, and filmmaking—with a little Dungeons & Dragons and professional wrestling thrown in for good measure.

staff.name: Lorenzo Edwards Jr
staff.bio: I am a Youth and Family Engagement Specialist with extensive experience across legal, educational and wellness sectors, including years of service as a guardian ad litem for children, educator for youth programs and yoga instructor. I prioritize meeting youth and families where they are and recognizing their inherent strengths, resilience and lived expertise. I am committed to fostering trust, supporting informed choice, and promoting meaningful engagement to help the youth and families achieve their highest possible level of independent functioning, stability, and help facilitate recovery.

staff.name: Aren Forrest
staff.bio:
Aren earned his Bachelors of Science in Psychology from the University of Wisconsin-Stevens Point and his Masters of Science in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Marquette University. He has experience working with youth, young adults and geriatric clients in community mental health, behavioral health, and private practice. Aren has worked to support people with a wide range of challenges from behavioral and emotional challenges, navigating neurodivergence, to major life changes. He has primarily worked with adolescents and young adults with depression, anxiety and identity concerns.

As a psychotherapist, Aren approaches his work with warmth, empathy and authenticity to cultivate a safe and supportive environment for clients to feel welcomed to come as they are. He enjoys bringing in creative elements such as art, music and nature to counseling to best fit each individual’s needs. He takes an integrative approach, primarily working through Acceptance and Commitment Therapy to assist people in finding a lifestyle in-line with what is important to them. He believes that true growth comes from a strong working relationship, the bravery to look at oneself, the vulnerability to show up as you are, and the courage to make a change for yourself.

Outside of his professional work, Aren enjoys spending time playing a wide variety of games, taking hikes in nature, playing disc golf, reading sci-fi and fantasy novels, and spending time with friends and family.

staff.name: Ernest Goggins
staff.bio:
Prevention, awareness, and education are at the forefront of the work I do as a Family Engagement Specialist for Inner Journey Consulting. My core values have always been to support families through evidence informed and culturally inclusive practices that decrease the many public health issues affecting his community, especially violence and systemic prejudice. 

I’m a product of Milwaukee, Wisconsin, having gone to Milwaukee Public Schools, before graduating from UW-Milwaukee with a B.A. in sociology. I’m a husband and father of two children, and have extensive experience in the field of prevention, including trauma informed care, crisis de-escalation, program development, youth services, parent education and facilitation. I have served my community since 2005, with a focus on its marginalized members.

As a new member of the IJC team, I am elated about the opportunity to learn from fellow colleagues, support the protective factors that families already possess while also providing comprehensive/interactive engagement to help decrease current or potential future risk factors in families. I’m a versatile professional and I’m able to work with a range of communities ("from jail to Yale"). I’m excited to help those who help others and to help families in Wisconsin thrive!

staff.name: Alex Herrmann
staff.bio:
With four years in wraparound teams, Alex Herrmann is a dedicated mental health rehab worker with skills in medication management, career and life skills development, psychoeducation, and peer support. Alex takes a holistic, person-centered approach, meeting clients where they are and supporting them as they move forward.

Blending clinical expertise with peer support values, Alex prioritizes active listening, adaptability, and humility. He’s always open to feedback and strives to create a comfortable, supportive space for clients. Being fully present—emotionally and mentally—is at the heart of his work.

Outside of work, Alex loves spending time outdoors with his wife, baby daughter, and border collie, embracing family life and new adventures. He believes in treating everyone with respect and dignity—both personally and professionally.

staff.name: Najwa Jaber
staff.bio:
Najwa earned her Bachelor’s degree in Social Work from the University of Wisconsin Milwaukee and will begin her Master’s in Social Work there this fall. She has lived in Milwaukee County her entire life and has always had a strong connection to her community. Najwa has spent years working with children in both social work settings and informal roles, and she continues to be passionate about supporting youth as they navigate life’s challenges.

As the oldest sibling in a large family, Najwa developed strong caregiving skills from a young age, which helped shape her path toward becoming a therapist. She is currently building her caseload and is accepting new clients.

Najwa enjoys working with youth and families on building emotional awareness, self-confidence, and healthy communication skills. She brings a culturally sensitive, strengths-based approach to her work and values meeting clients where they are.

In her free time, Najwa enjoys going on walks, trying new restaurants, attending local events, and spending time with her family and friends.

staff.name: LeLana Johnson
staff.bio: I am passionate about empowering youth to achieve their academic, personal, and career goals. With experience working with youth and young adults across various educational, career, and therapeutic settings, I have honed my ability to build strong relationships. My goal is to support and guide the next generation, helping them unlock their full potential and succeed in their pursuits.

staff.name: Sophia Korpal
staff.bio:
My passion for working with individuals struggling with mental illness began in high school while working in a residential treatment setting. After high school, I pursued an education where I could learn more about those I was already serving and what else I could do to help others. In 2017 I earned my Bachelor's of Social Work and Spanish through Winona State University in Winona MN, with a minor in child advocacy studies. I later moved to Milwaukee to continue my education graduating from UW-Milwaukee with a Master's in Social Work and Criminal Justice. I currently have my APSW working towards my LCSW.

My work in the field of mental health has been with individuals of all ages and I pride myself on my travels, both educational and independent, which have played a large part in my goal to be culturally diverse. I believe therapy is important to understanding one’s past in order to focus on obtaining a better future. Therapy can also help one process through current situations or to prepare for what is to come. My goal in providing therapy is to allow my clients to take the lead as they are the experts of their lives. My role is to provide them support and guidance and to get my clients to where they want to be.

The power of the human brain and body to recover, rewire, and continue fighting and building resilience is a beautiful thing. I have been able to experience other’s resilience and promote their perseverance in many settings. I have experience working with youth struggling with anxiety, depression, and with past and ongoing trauma in a day treatment setting, as well as with adults struggling to manage life stressors, trauma, and severe and persistent mental illness. I have also worked with individuals battling suicidal ideation due to depression, anxiety, PTSD, and persons suffering from delusions and hallucinations due to severe and persistent mental illness. My extensive experience working with individuals in mental health crises has provided me insight into the difficulties of obtaining outpatient treatment options, navigating inpatient treatment, and shown me that with support and a will to thrive people can remain in the community.

staff.name: Ashley Locke
staff.bio:
Ashley earned her Bachelor of Science in Psychology from Grand Canyon University and is currently pursuing her Master of Arts in Clinical Mental Health Counseling at Concordia University Irvine. She has experience working with youth and young adults in community mental health, university counseling, and behavioral health settings, supporting individuals with a wide range of emotional, behavioral, and life challenges.

As a Youth Support Specialist with Inner Journey Consulting, Ashley is passionate about helping youth build confidence, emotional awareness, resilience, and healthy coping skills. She values building genuine relationships and creating an environment where young people feel heard, supported, and encouraged as they navigate life’s challenges and work toward their goals.

Ashley approaches her work with empathy and a strengths-based perspective. She comes from a Christian background and is open to incorporating faith-based perspectives into support work when requested by clients and families.

Outside of her professional and academic work, Ashley enjoys spending time with family and friends, baking, trying new coffee shops and restaurants, reading, and spending time outdoors whenever she can.

staff.name: Leah Maas
staff.bio:
Leah received her bachelor's degree in Psychology from Concordia University of Wisconsin. She has experience working as a Registered Behavior Technician at an Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) therapy center for children with autism, fostering a positive, encouraging environment that supports the development of socially significant behaviors and reduces behavioral challenges. She is currently a graduate student at Colorado Christian University pursuing a master’s degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling and School Counseling. Additionally, she is an intern at the counseling center at Concordia University of Wisconsin, where she coordinates resources for students seeking counseling and supports students through crisis management.

She believes in creating a counseling space that is open, genuine, nonjudgmental, and compassionate. She values meeting individuals where they are and places strong importance on authentic connection in the counseling process. She has a growing interest in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Person-Centered Therapy. She enjoys supporting clients as they explore their thoughts, emotions, and strengths at their own pace.

In her personal time, Leah enjoys being active outdoors through hiking and kayaking, reading a good book, baking sourdough and sweets, cooking new recipes, and spending meaningful time with family and friends.

staff.name: Sage McCormick
staff.bio:
Hi, I’m Sage McCormick, and I am of Irish, Scottish, German, and Russian heritage. I have enjoyed working with people from all walks of life who need someone to walk with and support them. I have previously worked in community mental health care providing mental health services to victims of crime and underserved populations in Milwaukee. I specialize in taking a relational, cultural, and trauma-informed approach when working with people of all ages and from many walks of life. 

I received my bachelors in Women’s, Gender, and Sexuality Studies from the University of Montana, and my M.S. in Professional Counseling from Mount Mary University in Milwaukee. I practice from a Feminist and Community Activated Medicine orientation, combining my understanding of how systems and relational connections impact our health. I also am pursuing certification as a Clinical Rehabilitation Counselor to empower and support individuals with disabilities. I have worked with individuals navigating anxiety, trauma histories, relational wounds, LGBTQ+ identities, life transitions, and health concerns. In sessions, I enjoy bringing a mix of play, movement, nature, art, somatic, or mindfulness practices depending on the needs and likes of people I work with. I believe in taking a collaborative approach, as you are the expert of your experience, and I am along to support you in this vulnerable journey.  

I enjoy traveling and have been an avid traveler since I was 18 and have visited ten countries in total between Asia and Europe. I enjoy spending my free time with my dog, Belle, going on walks or kayaking. I also enjoy meals with my family, spending time with my nieces, horseback riding, and trying new experiences. If I’m not outside, I am likely reading fantasy or learning about folklore, trying a new craft, watching a movie, or baking.

staff.name: Paula Payne
staff.bio:
Paula is a Youth Support and Family Engagement Specialist with over 15 years of experience working in mental health, education, and social services settings.  She has a Masters degree in Community Counseling from Lakeland College and demonstrated success working as a director of before and after school programs, special education teacher and paraprofessional in MPS, youth counselor in residential treatment, supervised visitation worker supporting families engaged in child welfare, and caring for the elderly. 

Paula is passionate about advocating for and supporting families and is dedicated to helping individuals develop problem solving, communication, emotional awareness and regulations, as well as life or independent living skills.  She is also particularly interested in exploring values and character education as well as self-exploration and knowledge exercises.  

In her free time Paula enjoys travel (including a trip to Italy for a language immersion course), different genres of music and singing, cooking, spending time with her family, and researching her family history, considering herself an amateur genealogist.

Paula is excited to begin this new journey with Inner Journey and is available for Skills Development, Wellness Management, and Psycho-education services through Waukesha County CCS.

staff.name: Bethany Post
staff.bio:
Bethany Post is a Licensed Professional Counselor who has been working with children, adolescents, adults and families in an inpatient and intensive outpatient setting for 3 years. Bethany graduated from Capella University with her master’s in Clinical Mental Health counseling and a Bachelor’s from the University of Wisconsin Green Bay with a BS in Psychology and Human Development. Bethany has worked in a high acute setting developing relationships with patients and their families and finding what works best for them. She works with anxiety, depression, ADHD, personality disorders and other mental health needs.

Her approach blends evidence-based techniques with genuine empathy, helping the patient uncover their strengths and create meaningful change.

In her free time Bethany enjoys traveling, spending time with her husband and son and finding any opportunity to learn new, exciting things. Bethany embraces challenges and looks forward to the next adventure that awaits both personally and professionally.

staff.name: Brittiny Qualls
staff.bio:
Hi, my name is Brittiny Qualls. I am a mother, educator and skilled social worker with a deep commitment to helping individuals and families thrive. In 2020, I earned my Master’s in Social Work from Aurora University. Shortly after, while supporting parents and youth in their homes and advocating for children in the foster care system, I obtained my APSW and continued to strengthen my skills as a helping professional.

I bring over 20 years of experience, working with parents, families, children and adults. My training includes crisis intervention, trauma-informed care, person-centered therapy and strength-based approaches. Whether in the social work field or in the classroom, I strive to help individuals enhance their communication, emotional regulation and problem-solving skills.

My compassion and dedication have guided me in walking alongside those impacted by discrimination, trauma, foster care involvement, poverty, homelessness, unmet basic needs, mental health challenges, substance use, domestic violence and issues related to gender identity. From my teenage years volunteering with youth-serving organizations and the American Red Cross to my ongoing community involvement, I remain deeply connected to the needs and strengths of our local communities.

Being a proud Milwaukee native, I enjoy the beauty of all four Midwest seasons—especially summer. In my spare time, I love traveling, spending time with family, enjoying music and staying active. As a mother of teenagers, I hold a special place in my heart for youth and believe that every person holds a unique power within. My mission is to help individuals discover that power and support them in achieving their personal goals—so they can become the best versions of themselves.

staff.name: Rory Rhys
staff.bio:
Rory is a Youth Support Specialist who graduated from University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee with a Bachelor's degree in Psychology. He has years of experience working with Neurodivergent and LGBT+ youth in both educational and therapeutic settings as well as adolescents involved in the juvenile justice system.

Rory operates from a person-centered and trauma-informed philosophy of care, with particular interest in experiential or exposure therapy approaches and conflict de-escalation.

In his free time, Rory enjoys reading, drawing, and being out in nature.

staff.name: Shalimar Rydzik
staff.bio:
Hi, I’m Shal, an APSW Therapist with over 7 years of experience in the mental health field. My background includes adult and youth, individual and group therapy, clubhouse rehabilitation, partial hospitalization, wraparound and trauma-focused work. For the past 3 years, I’ve focused on working with youth and families within the Comprehensive Community Services (CCS) program. Before becoming a therapist, I spent many years in fast-paced, people-centered environments, building strong skills in communication, problem-solving, and understanding real- life stress. Combined with my personal experience as a parent and living with ADHD, I bring both professional training and real-world perspective into my work.

I work with children, teens, and families, with a focus on (but not limited to) ADHD, anxiety, depression and trauma. I also use DBT skills in a practical, relatable way that clients can apply in everyday life. My approach is person-centered, warm, and real. I use creative, hands-on, and nature-based approaches like art, movement, games, and outdoor sessions to help clients feel comfortable, build connection, and stay engaged. I’m known for looking beyond behaviors to understand the “why” underneath and identifying the barriers that need to be addressed first. I focus on building a strong foundation so that progress is meaningful and lasting.

I value working with families while also advocating for each client’s voice and individual experience. Clients and families often describe me as genuine, easy to connect with, and someone who truly cares. Outside of therapy, I’m a mom to teenagers, have three tiny dogs, and am working toward building a small hobby farm. I enjoy mixed media art, thrifting, a little trashy reality TV when there is time and all of the animals! All things that reflect the creative and down-to-earth energy I bring into my work.

staff.name: Samantha Scheffel
staff.bio:
Sam is currently studying psychology and social work with the goal of becoming a therapist. She is earning her degree through UW-Milwaukee and hopes to work primarily with children, although she is open to helping people of all ages and backgrounds. Sam likes to work in a strengths based and solution focused way, with genuine empathy, patience, and utmost compassion. She strives to create a comfortable and soothing environment for her clients that is judgement-free and supportive.

In her free time, Sam likes to read books of all genres, but particularly horror. She watches movies, scrapbooks, sketches, bakes, attends concerts, and loves to visit with her family. She lives with her fiancé and two cats, Miika and Maddie.

staff.name: Ethan Schmaling
staff.bio:
I was born and raised in a primarily rural community where Future Farmers of America (FFA) - now known as National FFA Organization - was a major part of my life. Growing up in that environment shaped my work ethic, my appreciation for agriculture, and my deep connection to the outdoors.

I attended Iowa State University, where I earned a degree in Agricultural Business and Economics with a minor in Political Science. After college, I spent seven years running research trials on swine vaccines. That experience taught me accountability, structure, and the importance of following through—skills I carry into my work today.

My approach to youth skill development is hands-on and experiential, with a strong emphasis on physical activity and nature-based services. I believe young people grow best when they are engaged, moving, and connected to their environment. Session activities are based on what the youth enjoys. I intentionally encourage youth to help guide our sessions—choosing the destination, setting goals, and taking ownership of the process.

I am a firm believer in the power of choice and accountability, whether that means deciding how we spend our time together or taking responsibility for everyday tasks. I enjoy hunting and fishing and cooking what I harvest. I strive to be a champion for the youth I work with. My role is to walk alongside them, support their voice, and remind them that we are in this together.

staff.name: Curtis Smith
staff.bio:
Curtis has a background working in many capacities within the fields of Education, Social Services and Therapy in and around Milwaukee. In the past, he has worked with at-risk youth, aspiring high school graduates, school parents and the developmentally disabled population.

In 2024, Curtis finished his Master’s degree in Marriage and Family Therapy, so he can work with couples as well as continue to work with youth. 

Curtis enjoys movies, music and the outdoors.

staff.name: Abby Sobczak
staff.bio:
Hi, I’m Abby. I’m a recent graduate with a Bachelor’s in Psychology with a minor in Justice Administration from Blackburn College in Southern Illinois. While in college, I worked as a supervisor for our local summer camp and in before- and after-school programs with children ranging from 5–12 years old (K–6th grade). In these roles, I enjoyed planning and leading outdoor activities and intentionally supporting children by recognizing and building on their individual strengths, confidence, and social skills.

During my time at Blackburn College, I played for the volleyball team and worked with the campus security team, experiences that strengthened my leadership, teamwork, and responsibility.

In my free time, I enjoy taking my dog, Fred, on new outdoor adventures, drawing, painting, baking, and spending time with my family.

staff.name: Maureen Villars
staff.bio:
Maureen “Mo” Villars, an Advanced Practice Social Worker, is honored to be part of Inner Journey’s team as a psychotherapist supporting clients of all ages. She has over 10 years of experience supporting individuals and families as they build resilience while navigating through the challenges of anxiety, depression, severe and persistent mental illness, personality disorders, and suicidality, to name a few.  

As an ADHD mom of children with Autism and ADHD, she has experience living in “chaos” (like a squirrel trying to corral a herd of baby squirrels).  Her humorous and nonjudgmental attitude supports clients who feel overwhelmed, unsure, and even skeptical. Trained in Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), Maureen uses and teaches skills to help families work toward a life that feels more manageable, more meaningful, and, yes, a lot calmer.

staff.name: Allison Wojnar
staff.bio:
Allison has supported both residential and inpatient behavioral health programs for adults, children, and adolescents. She also has 10 years of experience working with autistic children and their families. She is experienced in conducting sessions in the home, community, or in clinic-based settings. Allison has worked with individuals living with depression, anxiety, OCD, borderline personality disorder, schizophrenia, and substance use disorders. Allison has also conducted psychoeducational groups on CBT and DBT skills.

Professional interests include assent based and trauma informed care, neurodiversity, and harm reduction. 

In her free time, she enjoys traveling, trying new restaurants, swimming, reading, and being a cat mom.
